title: Bank Card
description: A simple resource/datapack and mod that turns the brown bundle into a bank card.
body:
# **Bank Card**

[![fabric](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@intergrav/devins-badges@3/assets/cozy/supported/fabric_vector.svg)](https://modrinth.com/datapack/bank-card/versions?l=fabric)
[![forge](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@intergrav/devins-badges@3/assets/cozy/supported/forge_vector.svg)](https://modrinth.com/datapack/bank-card/versions?l=forge)
[![neoforge](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Hyperbole-Devs/vectors/8494ec1ac495cfb481dc7e458356325510933eb0/assets/cozy/supported/neoforge_vector.svg)](https://modrinth.com/datapack/bank-card/versions?l=neoforge)
[![quilt](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@intergrav/devins-badges@3/assets/cozy/supported/quilt_vector.svg)](https://modrinth.com/datapack/bank-card/versions?l=quilt)

[![Minecraft: Resource Pack and Data Pack](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/e44cb1be49e1be3d325ecfc6db95deaf2331bcf2.png)](https://modrinth.com/datapack/bank-card/versions?l=datapack)

![Dynamic JSON Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/dynamic/json?url=https%3A%2F%2Fapi.modrinth.com%2Fv2%2Fproject%2FAiSc9qrC&query=downloads&prefix=Downloads%3A%20&logo=modrinth&label=Modrinth&color=green)


![Bank Card: Looking in Inventory](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/cd2030095224a45ec6007322c4c01c754c904876.jpeg)

_**Scroll to the bottom for translations.\
Role até o final para ver as traduções.\
Faites défiler jusqu'en bas pour les traductions.**_

### What is Bank Card?
It's a (very) simple resource pack that transforms the brown bundle into a bank card!
###  Wait... There's a brown bundle in the game? Isn't the default bundle already brown?
Yes, there is. I don't know why, but there is. Because it's useless i decided to re-texture it to a bank card.
### Why?
I created it because i once installed a mod that added money into Minecraft, and i thought it would be cool to add a bank card! Well, there weren't any mods that did that, so i created my own!
### Is there like a... a twist? A surprise? Something ACTUALLY useful?
Nope. Just that. Also by the way it's my first ever 'modifier' for Minecraft, so expect it to be very VERY simple. But, i'm trying to make a datapack that makes a custom crafting recipe for it.

⚠ Warning ⚠\
For people that download the resource/datapack: For some reason Modrinth does not show you what are the dependencies of resource/datapacks when you install them, so please check the dependencies! (Install P-x.y.z DATAPACK + P-x.y.z RESOURCEPACK ; Both files can be seen by clicking the version you want to install, then scrolling down).

## Crafting Recipe

![Bank Card: Crafting a Bank Card](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1218952bab95762b9554abea67dd2807e5c0c228.jpeg)

## Gallery

![Bank Card: Removing Paper](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/fdde01ce53f1fb24ec9c65d322da46356319772f.jpeg)

![Bank Card: Removing 5€ Banknote (Mod: New Currencies)](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6aa73b8ea8df5b406054c8256e9dcb9b20a0d3b8.jpeg)

![Bank Card: Removing 500€ Banknote (Mod: New Currencies)](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b61fd9f0da7d8da802795d8a68410155a09f2b9e.jpeg)

## Informations

<details>
<summary>Some informations about this project's versions</summary>
  
This project uses a similar system to Semantic Versioning: M/P-x.y.z

M/P : Mod / Pack (Resource/Datapack)

x : Major Version (Number changes when an update that adds/removes/modifies big features occurs)

y : Minor Version (Number changes when an update that adds/removes/modifies small features occurs)

z : Minecraft Update Version (Number changes when a Minecraft update occurs, but no actual changes to the resourcepack are made)

</details>

<details>
<summary>Some informations about Minecraft versions</summary>

In case i stop posting updates to the resource/datapack version, here's a quick tutorial on how to update the resource/datapack to the newest Minecraft version:

- Unzip the "bankcard_resourcepack_kakaunet.zip" and "bankcard_datapack_kakaunet.zip" folders.

- Inside the unzipped folders, find "pack.mcmeta" and open them using the notepad or any other app that allows you to edit text.

- Open Minecraft, and enter a world.

- Inside the world, open chat and type "/version".

- In the output in chat that appears, search for "pack_resource = <value1>", and remember the number located in <value1>.

- In the output in chat that appears, search for "pack_data = <value2>", and remember the number located in <value2>.

- Go back to the notepad, where both "pack.mcmeta" are open.

- In the "pack.mcmeta" from "bankcard_resourcepack_kakaunet", search for " "pack_format": <value3>, ", and replace the number located in <value3> with <value1> from before.

- In the "pack.mcmeta" from "bankcard_datapack_kakaunet", search for " "pack_format": <value4>, ", and replace the number located in <value4> with <value2> from before.

- Close the notepad, and zip the folders "bankcard_resourcepack_kakaunet" and "bankcard_datapack_kakaunet" (THE ONES WHO ARE NOT ZIPPED). _Warning: Check inside the newly zipped folders if "pack.mcmeta", "pack.png", and "assets"/"data" are the first things that you find inside! If not, find a way to make it so that they are the first things that you find inside, else the resource/datapack won't work!_

</details>


## Translations

<details>
<summary>🇫🇷 Traduction en Français / French Translation</summary>

# **Bank Card**

**⚠ Avertissement ⚠\
C'est assez difficile de traduire tout ça, alors il sera très peu probable que la description (en français) soit actualisé.**

![Bank Card: Looking in Inventory](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/cd2030095224a45ec6007322c4c01c754c904876.jpeg)

### Qu’est-ce que Bank Card ?
C’est un pack de ressources (très) simple qui transforme le bundle marron en carte bancaire !

### Attends… Il y a un bundle marron dans le jeu ? Le bundle par défaut n’est-il pas déjà marron ?
Oui, il y en a un. Je ne sais pas pourquoi, mais il existe. Comme il est inutile, j’ai décidé de le retexturer en carte bancaire.

### Pourquoi ?
Je l’ai créé parce qu’un jour j’ai installé un mod qui ajoutait de l’argent dans Minecraft, et j’ai pensé que ce serait cool d’ajouter une carte bancaire ! Eh bien, comme aucun mod ne le faisait, j’ai créé le mien !

### Y a-t-il un… un rebondissement ? Une surprise ? Quelque chose de VRAIMENT utile ?
Non. C’est tout. Au passage, c’est mon tout premier « modificateur » pour Minecraft, donc attendez-vous à quelque chose de très, TRÈS simple. Mais j’essaie de créer un datapack qui ajoute une recette de craft personnalisée pour elle.

**⚠ Avertissement ⚠\
Pour les personnes qui installent le resource/datapack: Je ne sais pas pourquoi, mais Modrinth ne vous montre pas les dépendances d’un resource/datapack quand vous le téléchargez, donc n’oubliez pas de vérifier le resource/datapack associé (Installez P-x.y.z DATAPACK + P-x.y.z RESOURCEPACK ; Les deux fichiers peuvent être trouvés dans la version que vous désirez installez, tout en bas).**

## Recette de craft

![Bank Card: Crafting a Bank Card](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1218952bab95762b9554abea67dd2807e5c0c228.jpeg)

![Bank Card: Removing 500€ Banknote (Mod: New Currencies)](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b61fd9f0da7d8da802795d8a68410155a09f2b9e.jpeg)

## Informations

<details>
<summary>Quelques informations sur les versions de ce projet</summary>
  
Ce projet utilise un système similaire au Semantic Versioning : M/P-x.y.z

R/D : Resourcepack / Datapack

x : Version majeure (le chiffre change lorsqu’une mise à jour ajoute/supprime/modifie de grandes fonctionnalités)

y : Version mineure (le chiffre change lorsqu’une mise à jour ajoute/supprime/modifie de petites fonctionnalités)

z : Version de mise à jour Minecraft (le chiffre change lorsqu’une mise à jour de Minecraft a lieu, mais qu’aucune modification réelle n’est apportée au resourcepack)

</details>

<details>
<summary>Quelques informations sur les versions de Minecraft</summary>

Au cas où j’arrêterais de publier des mises à jour de la version resource/datapack, voici un petit tutoriel sur la façon de le mettre à jour vers la dernière version de Minecraft :

- Décompressez les dossiers "bankcard_resourcepack_kakaunet.zip" et "bankcard_datapack_kakaunet.zip".

- Dans les dossiers décompressés, trouvez "pack.mcmeta" et ouvrez-les avec le bloc-notes ou toute autre application permettant d’éditer du texte.

- Ouvrez Minecraft et entrez dans un monde.

- Dans le monde, ouvrez le chat et tapez « /version ».

- Dans la sortie du chat qui apparaît, cherchez « pack_resource = <value1> » et notez le numéro situé dans <value1>.

- Dans la sortie du chat qui apparaît, cherchez « pack_data = <value2> » et notez le numéro situé dans <value2>.

- Retournez dans le bloc-notes, où les deux "pack.mcmeta" sont ouverts.

- Dans le "pack.mcmeta" de "bankcard_resourcepack_kakaunet", cherchez « "pack_format": <value3>, » et remplacez le numéro <value3> par <value1>.

- Dans le "pack.mcmeta" de "bankcard_datapack_kakaunet", cherchez « "pack_format": <value4>, » et remplacez le numéro <value4> par <value2>.

- Fermez le bloc-notes et compressez à nouveau les dossiers "bankcard_resourcepack_kakaunet" et "bankcard_datapack_kakaunet" (CEUX QUI NE SONT PAS ZIPPÉS). _Attention : vérifiez dans les nouveaux dossiers compressés que "pack.mcmeta", "pack.png" et "assets"/"data" sont les premiers fichiers visibles ! Sinon, assurez-vous qu’ils le soient, sinon le resource/datapack ne fonctionnera pas !_

</details>

</details>


<details>
<summary>🇧🇷 Tradução em Português (Brasil) / Portuguese (Brazil) Translation</summary>

# **Bank Card**

**⚠ Aviso ⚠\
É muito difícil traduzir tudo isso, então é altamente improvável que a descrição (em português) seja atualizada.**

![Bank Card: Looking in Inventory](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/cd2030095224a45ec6007322c4c01c754c904876.jpeg)

### O que é o Bank Card?
É um resource pack (muito) simples que transforma o bundle marrom em um cartão bancário!

### Espera… Existe mesmo um bundle marrom no jogo? O bundle padrão já não é marrom?
Sim, existe. Eu não sei por quê, mas existe. Como ele é inútil, decidi retexturizá-lo para virar um cartão bancário.

### Por quê?
Eu o criei porque uma vez instalei um mod que adicionava dinheiro ao Minecraft e achei que seria legal ter um cartão bancário! Bem, não havia nenhum mod que fizesse isso, então eu criei o meu!

### Tem algum… truque? Surpresa? Algo REALMENTE útil?
Não. Só isso. Ah, e é o meu primeiro “modificador” para Minecraft, então espere algo bem, BEM simples. Mas estou tentando fazer um datapack que crie uma receita de craft personalizada para ele.

**⚠ Aviso ⚠\
Para as pessoas que instalam o resource/datapack: Não sei por que, mas o Modrinth não mostra as dependências de um resource/datapack quando você o baixa, então não esqueça de verificar o resource/datapack relacionado (Instale P-x.y.z DATAPACK + P-x.y.z RESOURCEPACK ; Os dois arquivos podem ser encontrados dentro da versão que você quer instalar, lá embaixo).**

## Receita de Craft

![Bank Card: Crafting a Bank Card](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1218952bab95762b9554abea67dd2807e5c0c228.jpeg)

![Bank Card: Removing 500€ Banknote (Mod: New Currencies)](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b61fd9f0da7d8da802795d8a68410155a09f2b9e.jpeg)

## Informações

<details>
<summary>Algumas informações sobre as versões deste projeto</summary>
  
Este projeto usa um sistema semelhante ao Semantic Versioning: M/P-x.y.z

M/P : Mod / Pack (Resource/Datapack)

x : Versão Maior (o número muda quando uma atualização adiciona/remove/modifica grandes recursos)

y : Versão Menor (o número muda quando uma atualização adiciona/remove/modifica pequenos recursos)

z : Versão de Atualização do Minecraft (o número muda quando uma atualização do Minecraft ocorre, mas nenhuma mudança real é feita no resourcepack)

</details>

<details>
<summary>Algumas informações sobre as versões do Minecraft</summary>

Caso eu pare de postar atualizações para a versão resource/datapack, aqui vai um tutorial rápido de como atualizar o resource/datapack para a versão mais recente do Minecraft:

- Descompacte as pastas "bankcard_resourcepack_kakaunet.zip" e "bankcard_datapack_kakaunet.zip".

- Dentro das pastas descompactadas, encontre "pack.mcmeta" e abra-os usando o Bloco de Notas ou qualquer outro app que permita editar texto.

- Abra o Minecraft e entre em um mundo.

- Dentro do mundo, abra o chat e digite "/version".

- Na saída do chat que aparecer, procure por "pack_resource = <value1>" e anote o número localizado em <value1>.

- Na saída do chat que aparecer, procure por "pack_data = <value2>" e anote o número localizado em <value2>.

- Volte ao Bloco de Notas, onde ambos os "pack.mcmeta" estão abertos.

- No "pack.mcmeta" de "bankcard_resourcepack_kakaunet", procure por " "pack_format": <value3>, " e substitua o número em <value3> por <value1>.

- No "pack.mcmeta" de "bankcard_datapack_kakaunet", procure por " "pack_format": <value4>, " e substitua o número em <value4> por <value2>.

- Feche o Bloco de Notas e compacte novamente as pastas "bankcard_resourcepack_kakaunet" e "bankcard_datapack_kakaunet" (AS QUE NÃO ESTÃO ZIPADAS). _Atenção: verifique dentro das novas pastas zipadas se "pack.mcmeta", "pack.png" e "assets"/"data" são os primeiros arquivos visíveis! Caso contrário, dê um jeito de fazer com que eles sejam, senão o resource/datapack não funcionará!_

</details>


</details>
